58	// spawnReporterIfNeeded idempotently spawns the tide-reporter reader Job for a
59	// planner-level Job completion (Option C / T-09-13: AlreadyExists on Create is
60	// success; a pre-existing reporter Job means this completion was already
61	// observed). Returns isFirstCompletion=true when this is the initial
62	// observation of the planner Job's terminal state: either the reporter Job was
63	// newly spawned, or no ReporterImage is configured at all (stub/test path).
64	// When ReporterImage is set but the parent Project is unresolved, no spawn
65	// happens and isFirstCompletion stays false (mirrors the prior inline blocks
66	// in milestone_controller.go / phase_controller.go).
67	func spawnReporterIfNeeded(
68		ctx context.Context,
69		c client.Client,
70		scheme *runtime.Scheme,
71		parent metav1.Object,
72		project *tideprojectv1alpha1.Project,
73		parentKind string,
74		reporterImage string,
75	) (bool, error) {
76		logger := logf.FromContext(ctx)
77		if reporterImage == "" {
78			logger.V(1).Info("skipping reporter Job spawn: ReporterImage not configured",
79				"parentKind", parentKind, "parent", parent.GetName())
80			return true, nil
81		}
82		if project == nil {
83			return false, nil
84		}
85		reporterJobName := fmt.Sprintf("tide-reporter-%s", parent.GetUID())
86		var existing batchv1.Job
87		if gErr := c.Get(ctx, types.NamespacedName{Name: reporterJobName, Namespace: parent.GetNamespace()}, &existing); gErr != nil {
88			if !apierrors.IsNotFound(gErr) {
89				return false, fmt.Errorf("get reporter job %s: %w", reporterJobName, gErr)
90			}
91			// Not found — spawn it (first completion observation).
92			reporterJob := BuildReporterJob(parent, project, defaultSharedPVCName, string(parent.GetUID()), parentKind,
93				ReporterOptions{ReporterImage: reporterImage}, scheme)
94			if cErr := c.Create(ctx, reporterJob); cErr != nil {
95				if !apierrors.IsAlreadyExists(cErr) {
96					return false, fmt.Errorf("create reporter job %s: %w", reporterJobName, cErr)
97				}
98			} else {
99				logger.Info("spawned reporter Job", "job", reporterJobName,
100					"parentKind", parentKind, "parent", parent.GetName())
101			}
102			return true, nil
103		}
104		logger.V(1).Info("reporter Job already exists; skipping spawn (T-09-13)", "job", reporterJobName)
105		return false, nil
106	}
107	
108	// ProviderDefaults carries the Helm-chart-supplied defaults used as the
109	// last fallback in ResolveProvider's precedence chain (D-C2).
110	//
111	// Image is the default subagent image ref (vendor selection). Models maps
112	// level name ("milestone"|"phase"|"plan"|"task") to the per-level default
113	// model identifier. Both are filled at Manager startup from Helm values.
114	type ProviderDefaults struct {
115		// Image is the default subagent image ref. Empty string means
116		// "no Helm default" â caller is responsible for surfacing this
117		// at Job creation time (a missing image is a config error).
118		Image string
119	
120		// Models maps levelâmodel. Missing key means "no Helm default for
121		// that level".
122		Models map[string]string
123	}
124	
125	// ResolveProvider walks Project.Spec.Subagent precedence chain for the
126	// given level (D-C2). Returns a ProviderSpec with Vendor pinned to
127	// "anthropic" in v1.0 (per-vendor selection deferred â CONTEXT.md
128	// "Deferred Ideas"). Model and Params resolve via:
129	//
130	//	project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.<level>.Model â
131	//	project.Spec.Subagent.Model â
132	//	helmDefaults.Models[<level>] â
133	//	"" (caller surfaces missing-config error)
134	//
135	// Params merge: level Params copied first, then Project-level Params
136	// inserted only for keys NOT already set at the level â i.e., level
137	// wins on key conflict.
138	func ResolveProvider(project *tideprojectv1alpha1.Project, level string, helmDefaults ProviderDefaults) pkgdispatch.ProviderSpec {
139		// Helper to read per-level overrides.
140		var levelCfg *tideprojectv1alpha1.LevelConfig
141		if project != nil {
142			switch level {
143			case "milestone":
144				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Milestone
145			case "phase":
146				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Phase
147			case "plan":
148				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Plan
149			case "task":
150				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Task
151			}
152		}
153	
154		// Resolve Model.
155		var model string
156		switch {
157		case levelCfg != nil && levelCfg.Model != "":
158			model = levelCfg.Model
159		case project != nil && project.Spec.Subagent.Model != "":
160			model = project.Spec.Subagent.Model
161		default:
162			if helmDefaults.Models != nil {
163				model = helmDefaults.Models[level]
164			}
165		}
166	
167		// Merge Params â level overrides Project defaults on key conflict.
168		var params map[string]string
169		if levelCfg != nil && len(levelCfg.Params) > 0 {
170			params = make(map[string]string, len(levelCfg.Params))
171			maps.Copy(params, levelCfg.Params)
172		}
173		// (Project-level Params are not currently exposed on SubagentConfig
174		// â LevelConfig.Params is the per-level extension; if a future
175		// schema bump adds a top-level Subagent.Params, merge here with
176		// "level wins on conflict" semantics.)
177	
178		return pkgdispatch.ProviderSpec{
179			Vendor: "anthropic",
180			Model:  model,
181			Params: params,
182		}
183	}
184	
185	// BuildPlannerEnvelope constructs and marshals an EnvelopeIn for a
186	// planner-level dispatch. Mirrors task_controller.go:buildEnvelopeIn
187	// (Phase 2) but at the planner level: sets Role="planner",
188	// Level=<level>, populates Provider via ResolveProvider, and reuses the
189	// Caps / ProxyEndpoint / SignedToken parameters supplied by the caller.
190	//
191	// parent is the up-stack CRD whose UID stamps EnvelopeIn.TaskUID (the
192	// field is named TaskUID for backward-compat with Phase 2's envelope
193	// schema, but it carries the parent's UID at the planner level â the
194	// semantic is "the dispatch this envelope drives" regardless of level).
195	//
196	// prompt is the level-appropriate prompt body the dispatching reconciler
197	// supplies; it is assigned verbatim to EnvelopeIn.Prompt so the real
198	// subagent's template render has the actual outcome to author against
199	// (defect #4: prior to this the field was never set and the real Claude
200	// planner saw an empty prompt). token and prompt are DISTINCT params â
201	// token is the credproxy HMAC, prompt is the authoring instruction. The
202	// project planner passes Project.Spec.OutcomePrompt; deeper planners pass
203	// the same outcome (the parent artifact context â MILESTONE.md, the phase
204	// brief, PLAN.md â lives on the PVC and the template instructs reading it,
205	// and ParentName flows through EnvelopeIn.Dispatch).
206	// outcomePromptOf returns project.Spec.OutcomePrompt, nil-safe: the deeper
207	// reconcilers resolve the owning Project by walking the parent chain
208	// (resolveProject / resolveProjectForPlan), which returns nil on a not-yet-
209	// visible chain. A nil project yields "" — the same empty-prompt shape the
210	// real subagent already guards (EMPTY_PROMPT warning) rather than a panic.
211	func outcomePromptOf(project *tideprojectv1alpha1.Project) string {
212		if project == nil {
213			return ""
214		}
215		return project.Spec.OutcomePrompt
216	}
217	
218	func BuildPlannerEnvelope(level string, parent metav1.Object, project *tideprojectv1alpha1.Project, attempt int, token, prompt string, caps pkgdispatch.Caps, proxyEndpoint string, helmDefaults ProviderDefaults) (pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn, []byte, error) {
219		envIn := pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn{
220			APIVersion:    pkgdispatch.APIVersionV1Alpha1,
221			Kind:          pkgdispatch.KindTaskEnvelopeIn,
222			TaskUID:       string(parent.GetUID()),
223			Role:          "planner",
224			Level:         level,
225			Prompt:        prompt,
226			Caps:          caps,
227			ProxyEndpoint: proxyEndpoint,
228			SignedToken:   token,
229			Provider:      ResolveProvider(project, level, helmDefaults),
230		}
231	
232		// Stamp parentName into the dedicated Dispatch metadata field so the stub
233		// planner can populate the child *Ref field (e.g. milestoneRef, phaseRef)
234		// without querying the K8s API â parent.GetName() is the authoritative
235		// source (T-07-03-03: parentName is metadata, not a secret). This is kept
236		// out of Provider.Params (model-parameters-only) so the anthropic runner's
237		// strict param allow-list is not tripped by dispatch metadata.
238		envIn.Dispatch = &pkgdispatch.DispatchMeta{ParentName: parent.GetName()}
239	
240		data, err := json.Marshal(envIn)
241		if err != nil {
242			return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn{}, nil, fmt.Errorf("marshal planner envelope: %w", err)
243		}
244		return envIn, data, nil
245	}
246	
247	// MaterializeChildCRDs delegates to reporter.MaterializeChildCRDs.
248	// The materialization machinery lives in internal/reporter so cmd/tide-reporter
249	// can import it without a back-edge to internal/controller (plan 09-04).
250	func MaterializeChildCRDs(ctx context.Context, c client.Client, scheme *runtime.Scheme, parent metav1.Object, children []pkgdispatch.ChildCRDSpec) error {
251		return reporter.MaterializeChildCRDs(ctx, c, scheme, parent, children)
252	}
253	
254	// resolveImage walks Project.Spec.Subagent precedence chain for the given
255	// level, returning the resolved subagent container image reference.
256	//
257	//	Levels.<level>.Image → Spec.Subagent.Image → helmDefaults.Image → ""
258	//
259	// An empty return means no image was configured; callers must surface this
260	// as a config error rather than dispatching a Job with an empty image field.
261	// Level "project" has no entry in the switch (the CRD has no Levels.Project);
262	// resolution falls straight to Spec.Subagent.Image, then helmDefaults.Image.
263	func resolveImage(project *tideprojectv1alpha1.Project, level string, helmDefaults ProviderDefaults) string {
264		var levelCfg *tideprojectv1alpha1.LevelConfig
265		if project != nil {
266			switch level {
267			case "milestone":
268				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Milestone
269			case "phase":
270				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Phase
271			case "plan":
272				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Plan
273			case "task":
274				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Task
275			}
276		}
277		switch {
278		case levelCfg != nil && levelCfg.Image != "":
279			return levelCfg.Image
280		case project != nil && project.Spec.Subagent.Image != "":
281			return project.Spec.Subagent.Image
282		default:
283			return helmDefaults.Image
284		}
285	}
286	
287	// checkParentApproval implements the D-02 descent hold — children materialize
288	// but dispatch waits for parental approval (tidal lock pending).
289	//
290	// Returns (true, nil) when the direct parent is parked at AwaitingApproval,
291	// signalling that the child reconciler must hold Job dispatch with a 5s requeue.
292	// Returns (false, nil) when parentName is empty (root level — no parent to check)
293	// or when the parent is not found (client.IgnoreNotFound — NotFound is transient
294	// informer lag; callers continue dispatch and the next reconcile re-checks).
295	// Non-NotFound Get errors propagate to the standard requeue-on-error path.
296	//
297	// The parentKind switch covers "Milestone", "Phase", and "Plan" — the three
298	// parent kinds that can park at AwaitingApproval. Unknown kinds return (false, nil)
299	// (permissive default: unknown parent kind should not block dispatch).
300	//
301	// Design note: held children stay at Status.Phase="" — this helper writes NO
302	// status so tide approve's findAwaiting* cannot target a held child instead of
303	// the parked parent (Pitfall 5 from 12-RESEARCH.md).
304	func checkParentApproval(ctx context.Context, c client.Client, ns, parentName, parentKind string) (bool, error) {
305		if parentName == "" {
306			return false, nil
307		}
308		switch parentKind {
309		case "Milestone":
310			var ms tideprojectv1alpha1.Milestone
311			if err := c.Get(ctx, client.ObjectKey{Namespace: ns, Name: parentName}, &ms); err != nil {
312				return false, client.IgnoreNotFound(err)
313			}
314			return ms.Status.Phase == "AwaitingApproval", nil
315		case "Phase":
316			var ph tideprojectv1alpha1.Phase
317			if err := c.Get(ctx, client.ObjectKey{Namespace: ns, Name: parentName}, &ph); err != nil {
318				return false, client.IgnoreNotFound(err)
319			}
320			return ph.Status.Phase == "AwaitingApproval", nil
321		case "Plan":
322			var plan tideprojectv1alpha1.Plan
323			if err := c.Get(ctx, client.ObjectKey{Namespace: ns, Name: parentName}, &plan); err != nil {
324				return false, client.IgnoreNotFound(err)
325			}
326			return plan.Status.Phase == "AwaitingApproval", nil
327		}
328		return false, nil
329	}

17	package dispatch
18	
19	// ProviderSpec selects the LLM vendor + model + per-vendor tuning knobs the
20	// subagent image should use to satisfy a single dispatch (D-C3). It is carried
21	// on every EnvelopeIn so vendor + model are orthogonal axes from the
22	// orchestrator's point of view: the dispatching reconciler resolves
23	// Project.Spec.subagent.levels.{level}.{vendor,model,params} → Project-level
24	// defaults → Helm-chart defaults and stamps the resolved triple here. The
25	// provider-side Subagent implementation (e.g. internal/subagent/anthropic)
26	// reads Provider.Model to drive its CLI invocation and rejects an envelope
27	// whose Provider.Vendor does not match the image's compiled-in sentinel —
28	// fail-fast defense against config drift between image tag and envelope
29	// content.
30	//
31	// Params is intentionally a string→string map (not a typed struct) so future
32	// per-vendor knobs (Anthropic thinking-budget, OpenAI temperature, Google
33	// safetySettings, etc.) can land without a pkg/dispatch schema bump every
34	// time. Validation of Params keys/values is the provider implementation's
35	// responsibility — pkg/dispatch only guarantees the bytes round-trip.
36	type ProviderSpec struct {
37		// Vendor is the provider sentinel string the subagent image checks at
38		// startup. Canonical values: "anthropic", "openai", "google", "xai",
39		// "opencode". Required (no omitempty) — every dispatch declares a vendor.
40		Vendor string `json:"vendor"`
41	
42		// Model is the per-vendor model identifier passed to the provider CLI or
43		// SDK (e.g. "claude-opus-4-7", "claude-sonnet-4-6", "claude-haiku-4-5",
44		// "o1-mini"). Required (no omitempty).
45		Model string `json:"model"`
46	
47		// Params is the per-vendor tuning passthrough. Keys and values are
48		// vendor-specific; pkg/dispatch performs no semantic validation. Omitted
49		// from JSON when empty so dispatches that pass no tuning don't carry a
50		// "params: {}" placeholder.
51		Params map[string]string `json:"params,omitempty"`
52	}

18	
19	import "context"
20	
21	// Subagent is the out-of-tree contract for TIDE subagent image runtimes
22	// (D-A1 / REQ-SUB-01). Out-of-tree image authors implement this interface to
23	// integrate a custom LLM backend into TIDE without modifying the orchestrator.
24	//
25	// In-repo reference implementations:
26	//   - cmd/stub-subagent — canned test responses, no LLM call (Plan 04).
27	//   - internal/harness  — cap enforcement + signed-token client + secret
28	//     redaction + output-path validation (Plan 06). The harness wraps a
29	//     concrete Subagent implementation selected at startup.
30	//
31	// The concrete Anthropic-backed implementation (Phase 3) satisfies this
32	// interface behind the provider firewall enforced by `make verify-dispatch-imports`.
33	type Subagent interface {
34		// Run executes the task described by in and returns the result envelope.
35		// The context deadline should be set to caps.wallClockSeconds + grace; the
36		// harness also enforces the cap internally via SIGTERM (HARN-02).
37		//
38		// A non-nil error indicates a dispatch-level failure (network, I/O);
39		// task-level failures (subagent exited non-zero) are expressed via
40		// EnvelopeOut.ExitCode and EnvelopeOut.Reason.
41		Run(ctx context.Context, in EnvelopeIn) (EnvelopeOut, error)
42	}

17	// Command claude-subagent — Phase 3 real-Claude swap of stub-subagent. Thin
18	// shim: load EnvelopeIn → anthropic.Run → write EnvelopeOut. Ignores the
19	// envelope's Dev struct (PATTERNS.md line 442 / D-F1).
20	package main
21	
22	import (
23		"context"
24		"encoding/json"
25		"flag"
26		"fmt"
27		"io"
28		"os"
29		"os/signal"

31		"syscall"
32		"time"
33

35		"github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/internal/harness"
36		"github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/internal/subagent/anthropic"
37		pkgdispatch "github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/pkg/dispatch"
38	)
39	
40	// parsePricingOverridesFromEnv reads TIDE_PRICING_OVERRIDES_JSON and parses it.
41	// An empty or missing env var returns a nil map (no overrides). An invalid JSON
42	// value logs a loud stderr warning and returns nil (bad override must not fail
43	// the session — Plan 14-05 validates at manager startup; this is defense in depth).
44	func parsePricingOverridesFromEnv(stderr io.Writer) map[string]pkgdispatch.PriceOverride {
45		raw := os.Getenv("TIDE_PRICING_OVERRIDES_JSON")
46		if raw == "" {
47			return nil
48		}
49		overrides, err := pkgdispatch.ParsePricingOverrides(raw)
50		if err != nil {
51			fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "pricing: invalid TIDE_PRICING_OVERRIDES_JSON, ignoring overrides: %v\n", err)
52			return nil
53		}
54		return overrides
55	}
56	
57	type anthropicRunner interface {
58		Run(ctx context.Context, in pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn) (pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut, error)
59	}
60	
61	var newSubagent = func(
62		claudeBinary, workspaceRoot string,
63		pricingOverrides map[string]pkgdispatch.PriceOverride,
64	) anthropicRunner {
65		return anthropic.New(anthropic.Options{
66			ClaudeBinary:     claudeBinary,
67			WorkspaceRoot:    workspaceRoot,
68			PricingOverrides: pricingOverrides,
69		})
70	}
71	var ensureWorktreeFunc = harness.EnsureWorktree
72	var commitWorktreeFunc = func(worktreeDir, taskUID string) (plumbing.Hash, bool, error) {
73		return harness.CommitWorktree(worktreeDir, taskUID)
74	}
75	
76	func main() {
77		fs := flag.NewFlagSet("claude-subagent", flag.ExitOnError)
78		envelopePath := fs.String("envelope-path", "",
79			"path to EnvelopeIn JSON (default: $TIDE_ENVELOPE_PATH or /workspace/envelopes/$TIDE_TASK_UID/in.json)")
80		workspaceRoot := fs.String("workspace-root", "/workspace", "PVC mount root")
81		if err := fs.Parse(os.Args[1:]); err != nil {
82			os.Exit(2)
83		}
84		// Resolve envelope path: flag → $TIDE_ENVELOPE_PATH → per-task-uid default.
85		// This MUST match stub-subagent (cmd/stub-subagent/main.go) and the
86		// controller's write location, which is /workspace/envelopes/$TIDE_TASK_UID/in.json
87		// (the controller passes no --envelope-path arg, so it relies on this default).
88		// The previous hardcoded /workspace/envelopes/in.json default did not include
89		// the per-task-uid subdirectory, so the real claude planner/executor could never
90		// find its input envelope. Only surfaced in live real-Claude runs — CI exercises

# Milestone 03 — Heterogeneous Provider Dispatch (Codex Runtime)

**Project:** dogfood-codex-runtime
**Slug:** `milestone-03-hetero-integration`
**Status:** Planned — phases dispatched

---

## Outcome Statement

A TIDE operator configures a single Project whose **planner pool runs Claude**
and whose **executor pool runs Codex (OpenAI)**, applies it, and watches a
multi-phase run dispatch to completion across both providers in one set of
Project-scoped waves. Mixed-provider waves actually execute — not interface
scaffolding — and the wave-boundary failure contract is byte-for-byte identical
regardless of which provider ran a task. Codex spend is attributed correctly on
the Telemetry tab and counts against the same budget cap as Claude spend.

The only new product surface is the **Codex subagent package**
(`internal/subagent/codex/`) plus the **per-level vendor switch** in the
controller. Everything else — the global Execution DAG, wave derivation, global
dispatch/failure/gates/resumption, cost/budget/telemetry/cache, and the
dashboard — already ships in main and is consumed unchanged.

---

## Architecture Decisions (locked — do not reinvent in phases)

### D1 — Implementation location mirrors `internal/subagent/anthropic/` exactly

`internal/subagent/codex/` ships `client.go` (Client + constructor), `run.go`
(`Subagent.Run(ctx, EnvelopeIn) (EnvelopeOut, error)`), a JSONL `stream_parser.go`,
`pricing.go`, `doc.go` (references the D-C1 layering pattern), and `Dockerfile`
(multi-stage, mirrors `anthropic/Dockerfile`). All OpenAI/Codex-specific code
stays behind the `pkg/dispatch.Subagent` interface. **No Codex imports** in
`internal/controller/` or `cmd/manager/` — enforced by the existing provider
import firewall (`make verify-import-firewall`, `verify-dispatch-imports`).

### D2 — Per-level runtime selection (not per-task, not per-project)

The planner pool and the executor pool each pin a runtime via the existing
`Spec.Subagent.Levels.{milestone,phase,plan,task}` overrides. This is the
correct granularity: it matches the spec's separate-pools argument, achieves
real heterogeneity within one run, and avoids the API/scheduler complexity of
per-task selection. Rejected alternatives (do not reopen): per-task selection
(large API surface, scheduler churn) and per-project selection (no mixing within
a run, defeats the goal).

Today `resolveImage` already honors a per-level image, but `ResolveProvider`
(`internal/controller/dispatch_helpers.go`) returns `Vendor:"anthropic"`
unconditionally — so a Codex image is *selected* at a level but then *rejected*
at runtime by the image's vendor sentinel. **Wiring the per-level vendor switch
is part of this milestone.** Vendor resolves down the same precedence chain as
model/image: `Levels.<level>.Vendor → Spec.Subagent.Vendor → Helm default →
"anthropic"`. The Codex subagent's compiled-in sentinel is `"openai"`.

### D3 — Credential path: Secret-ref only, env-key auth

`OPENAI_API_KEY` (or `CODEX_API_KEY`) is delivered via a same-namespace K8s
Secret using the established `providerSecretRef` pattern. Never mount host
config, never headless OAuth (broken upstream: claude-code#29983, #7100 — the
same restriction applies to Codex). Secret material is **referenced by name
only**; chart values carry the Secret name, never the key bytes.

### D4 — Mixed-provider waves: identical wave-boundary failure semantics

The spec's "Failure handling at wave boundaries" applies unchanged across
providers and is already enforced by the global indegree model:

- Failed task → siblings in the same wave continue (declared independent).
- Dependents in later waves → never dispatched (indegree never reaches zero).
- Non-dependents in later waves → dispatched in strict profile.

This contract **must not change** when tasks run on different providers. No
parallel failure path is added — the global indegree model is the single
source of truth.

### D5 — Usage accounting + cost are required, not optional

The Codex `Run()` normalizes the OpenAI usage block into `pkg/dispatch.Usage`,
or budget enforcement, `tide_cost_cents_total`, and the cache-efficiency panel
all read $0/blank for Codex:

- `InputTokens`  ← `usage.prompt_tokens`
- `OutputTokens` ← `usage.completion_tokens`
- `CacheReadTokens` ← `usage.prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens`
  (OpenAI caching is automatic, marker-free, 1,024-token floor — there is no
  `cache_control` to set and no separate "cache creation" concept, so
  **`CacheCreationTokens` stays 0**, unlike Anthropic.)
- `EstimatedCostCents` + `CacheSavingsCents` ← a Codex-side pricing table
  mirroring `anthropic/pricing.go` (per-model cents/MTok; `cacheRead` is
  OpenAI's discounted cached-input rate).

The locked metric label set stays `{project,phase,plan,wave}`; the `task` label
remains **forbidden** (metriccardinality analyzer). The provider-neutral
`SharedContext` field on `EnvelopeIn` is plain Go struct state — Codex receives
it for free, no special handling.

### D6 — Codex CLI invocation (carried verbatim — do not re-research)

`codex exec` is first-class headless (one-shot, no TTY, final message to
stdout). Container-clean flags: `--ephemeral --skip-git-repo-check
--ignore-user-config`. `--json` emits a JSONL event stream (parsed for the
usage block + final message). `--output-schema <file>` constrains the final
response to a JSON schema — used for the mandated JSON child-CRD emission.
Sandbox defaults read-only; the runner sets `--sandbox workspace-write` (safe
inside an already-isolated pod, mirrors the Claude runner posture). Auth is
env-key (`OPENAI_API_KEY`). Image name mirrors the Claude convention:
`ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent` (exact tag/registry are authoring
decisions).

---

## Phase Decomposition

Six phases. All cross-phase interface contracts — the `"openai"` vendor
sentinel, the `Usage` field mapping, the vendor precedence chain, the image
name, and the wave-failure contract — are locked in this document, so the
planning DAG is shallow. Phase 01 (pricing/usage) and phase 04 (vendor switch)
have no dependencies and dispatch together; phase 02 builds on 01, phase 05 on
04, phase 03 on 02; phase 06 is the milestone-wide join barrier.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    P1[phase-01-codex-pricing-usage]
    P2[phase-02-codex-subagent-core]
    P3[phase-03-codex-image-entrypoint]
    P4[phase-04-per-level-vendor-switch]
    P5[phase-05-chart-secret-wiring]
    P6[phase-06-hetero-integration]
    P1 --> P2
    P2 --> P3
    P4 --> P5
    P2 --> P6
    P3 --> P6
    P4 --> P6
    P5 --> P6
```

### phase-01-codex-pricing-usage — *(no dependencies)*

Pure-Go foundation for Codex cost/usage. Adds `internal/subagent/codex/pricing.go`
(per-model cents/MTok table mirroring `anthropic/pricing.go`; `cacheRead` is the
discounted cached-input rate; no `cacheWrite` concept) and the usage-normalization
helper that maps an OpenAI usage block into `pkg/dispatch.Usage` per D5
(`cached_tokens → CacheReadTokens`, `CacheCreationTokens` fixed at 0,
`EstimatedCostCents` + `CacheSavingsCents` computed). Deliverable boundary:
`pricing_test.go` proves the table and cost math offline, including the
1,024-token cache floor and the zero-cache-creation invariant. No CLI, no
container, no controller touch.

### phase-02-codex-subagent-core — *(depends on: phase-01-codex-pricing-usage)*

The core `internal/subagent/codex/` package behind `pkg/dispatch.Subagent`:
`client.go` (Client + constructor), `run.go` (`Run` shells `codex exec` with the
D6 flag set, schema-constrained child-CRD emission via `--output-schema`),
`stream_parser.go` (JSONL event stream → final message + usage block),
`doc.go`. Fail-fast vendor check: refuse any envelope whose `Provider.Vendor !=
"openai"`, mirroring the anthropic sentinel guard. Populates `Usage` via the
phase-01 helper. Deliverable boundary: `Run` unit-tested against a fake `codex`
binary / fixture JSONL stream — fully offline; any real OpenAI call is guarded
behind `OPENAI_API_KEY` being set. Child-CRD read/traversal defenses mirror
anthropic's. No controller, no chart, no `cmd/` changes.

### phase-03-codex-image-entrypoint — *(depends on: phase-02-codex-subagent-core)*

The runtime image and its entrypoint. Adds `cmd/codex-subagent/main.go` (thin
shim: load `EnvelopeIn` → `codex.Run` → write `EnvelopeOut`, mirroring
`cmd/claude-subagent/main.go`, harness-wrapped) and
`internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile` (multi-stage build bundling the Codex CLI
binary, mirroring `anthropic/Dockerfile`). Deliverable boundary: the Dockerfile
builds clean; the entrypoint round-trips a fixture envelope through the package
offline; image-build/load tooling references
`ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent`. Imports the phase-02 package only.

### phase-04-per-level-vendor-switch — *(no dependencies)*

The controller-side vendor switch — no Codex imports. Adds a `Vendor` field to
`SubagentConfig`/`LevelConfig` (CEL-validated, optional, defaults to
`"anthropic"`), regenerates CRDs/deepcopy, and rewires `ResolveProvider`
(`dispatch_helpers.go`) to resolve vendor down the precedence chain
`Levels.<level>.Vendor → Spec.Subagent.Vendor → Helm default → "anthropic"`,
replacing the unconditional `Vendor:"anthropic"`. Threads the Helm vendor
default through `ProviderDefaults` + `cmd/manager/env.go`. Deliverable boundary:
unit tests prove a Codex level resolves `Vendor:"openai"` while a Claude level
stays `"anthropic"` in the same Project; `make manifests`/`generate` clean;
`verify-import-firewall` + `verify-dispatch-imports` green (zero provider
leakage). The interface contract — the `"openai"` sentinel string — matches
phase-02 by the lock in this doc, not by a code edge.

### phase-05-chart-secret-wiring — *(depends on: phase-04-per-level-vendor-switch)*

Helm chart + operator-facing config. Adds the `images.codexSubagent` /
`subagent.defaults` block for the Codex image, the new provider-Secret **name**
value for `OPENAI_API_KEY`/`CODEX_API_KEY` (referenced by name only — never the
key bytes), the env-key injection into dispatch Job pods following the
`providerSecretRef` pattern, and the manager-Deployment env that feeds the
phase-04 Helm vendor default. Deliverable boundary: `helm template` renders the
codex image default, the Secret ref, and the vendor-default env across
enabled/disabled permutations; no secret material inlined. Consumes the phase-04
manager-env contract.

### phase-06-hetero-integration — *(depends on: phase-02, phase-03, phase-04, phase-05)*

The milestone's join barrier — authors no new feature scope. Adds the
heterogeneous example Project (`examples/projects/.../project.yaml`: planner
pool Claude, executor pool Codex) and the mixed-provider integration test that
dispatches a multi-phase run to completion across both providers in one set of
Project-scoped waves. Verifies, end-to-end: (1) the wave-boundary failure
contract (D4) is identical when the failed task ran on Codex — siblings
continue, dependents never dispatch, non-dependents dispatch in strict profile;
(2) Codex spend is attributed on `tide_cost_cents_total` + the cache-efficiency
panel under the locked label set, and counts against the budget cap; (3)
`make test` exits 0 and every `make verify-*` guard is green. Fixes whatever the
join surfaces.

---

## Exit Criteria

1. `make test` exits 0 — all existing unit and integration tests pass unmodified.
2. `make verify-import-firewall` and `make verify-dispatch-imports` are green:
   no `internal/subagent/codex` (or OpenAI SDK) import appears in
   `internal/controller/` or `cmd/manager/`.
3. In one Project, `ResolveProvider` returns `Vendor:"openai"` for a
   Codex-pinned level and `Vendor:"anthropic"` for a Claude-pinned level
   (unit test).
4. The Codex `Run()` rejects an envelope whose `Provider.Vendor != "openai"`
   (fail-fast vendor guard, unit test).
5. The Codex usage normalizer maps `cached_tokens → CacheReadTokens`, holds
   `CacheCreationTokens == 0`, and the pricing table computes
   `EstimatedCostCents`/`CacheSavingsCents` offline (unit test).
6. `internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile` builds clean and bundles the Codex CLI.
7. The mixed-provider integration run reaches completion; a forced Codex task
   failure reproduces the D4 wave-boundary semantics byte-for-byte.
8. `tide_cost_cents_total` and the cache-efficiency panel attribute non-zero
   Codex spend under the `{project,phase,plan,wave}` label set; the `task` label
   is absent (metriccardinality analyzer green).
9. No OpenAI API call runs in tests unless `OPENAI_API_KEY` is set — the suite
   passes fully offline.
10. No secret material is inlined in any manifest or chart value — credentials
    are referenced by Secret name only.

---

## Constraints (inherited)

- All Codex/OpenAI-specific code stays in `internal/subagent/codex/` behind the
  `pkg/dispatch.Subagent` interface — no provider leakage into the controller
  or `cmd/manager`.
- Wave-boundary failure semantics unchanged for mixed-provider waves; no
  parallel failure path — the global indegree model is authoritative.
- Secret material never inlined in manifests or chart values — Secret name only.
- Apache 2.0 headers on all new files, logr/zap logging, CEL validation for new
  CRD fields, controller-runtime patterns throughout; keep all `make verify-*`
  guards green.
- Do not break existing tests; confirm `make test` is green before any plan is
  delivered.
- No real OpenAI API call in tests without an explicit `OPENAI_API_KEY` env
  guard — tests pass offline.

---

## Upstream / Downstream Milestones

- **Upstream:** v1.0.2 "Spring Tide" + late v1.0.1 — the v1alpha2 schema +
  global Execution DAG (Phases 23–26), global dispatch/failure/gates/resumption
  (Phase 25), cost/budget/telemetry/cache (Phases 16, 18–21), and the dashboard
  global execution-DAG view (Phase 26). All consumed as given infrastructure.
- **Downstream:** None currently scoped. The vendor switch generalizes to
  additional providers (Google, xAI) by adding a subagent package + pricing
  table behind the same interface — no controller change required.
MILESTONE_EOF

scaffolding — and the wave-boundary failure contract is byte-for-byte identical
regardless of which provider ran a task. Codex spend is attributed correctly on
the Telemetry tab and counts against the same budget cap as Claude spend.

The only new product surface is the **Codex subagent package**
(`internal/subagent/codex/`) plus the **per-level vendor switch** in the
controller. Everything else — the global Execution DAG, wave derivation, global
dispatch/failure/gates/resumption, cost/budget/telemetry/cache, and the
dashboard — already ships in main and is consumed unchanged.

### D1 — Implementation location mirrors `internal/subagent/anthropic/` exactly

`internal/subagent/codex/` ships `client.go` (Client + constructor), `run.go`
(`Subagent.Run(ctx, EnvelopeIn) (EnvelopeOut, error)`), a JSONL `stream_parser.go`,
`pricing.go`, `doc.go` (references the D-C1 layering pattern), and `Dockerfile`
(multi-stage, mirrors `anthropic/Dockerfile`). All OpenAI/Codex-specific code
stays behind the `pkg/dispatch.Subagent` interface. **No Codex imports** in
`internal/controller/` or `cmd/manager/` — enforced by the existing provider
import firewall (`make verify-import-firewall`, `verify-dispatch-imports`).

### D2 — Per-level runtime selection (not per-task, not per-project)

The planner pool and the executor pool each pin a runtime via the existing
`Spec.Subagent.Levels.{milestone,phase,plan,task}` overrides. This is the
correct granularity: it matches the spec's separate-pools argument, achieves
real heterogeneity within one run, and avoids the API/scheduler complexity of
per-task selection. Rejected alternatives (do not reopen): per-task selection
(large API surface, scheduler churn) and per-project selection (no mixing within
a run, defeats the goal).

Today `resolveImage` already honors a per-level image, but `ResolveProvider`
(`internal/controller/dispatch_helpers.go`) returns `Vendor:"anthropic"`
unconditionally — so a Codex image is *selected* at a level but then *rejected*
at runtime by the image's vendor sentinel. **Wiring the per-level vendor switch
is part of this milestone.** Vendor resolves down the same precedence chain as
model/image: `Levels.<level>.Vendor → Spec.Subagent.Vendor → Helm default →
"anthropic"`. The Codex subagent's compiled-in sentinel is `"openai"`.

### D3 — Credential path: Secret-ref only, env-key auth

`OPENAI_API_KEY` (or `CODEX_API_KEY`) is delivered via a same-namespace K8s
Secret using the established `providerSecretRef` pattern. Never mount host
config, never headless OAuth (broken upstream: claude-code#29983, #7100 — the
same restriction applies to Codex). Secret material is **referenced by name
only**; chart values carry the Secret name, never the key bytes.
EOF

### D4 — Mixed-provider waves: identical wave-boundary failure semantics

The spec's "Failure handling at wave boundaries" applies unchanged across
providers and is already enforced by the global indegree model:

- Failed task → siblings in the same wave continue (declared independent).
- Dependents in later waves → never dispatched (indegree never reaches zero).
- Non-dependents in later waves → dispatched in strict profile.

This contract **must not change** when tasks run on different providers. No
parallel failure path is added — the global indegree model is the single
source of truth.

### D5 — Usage accounting + cost are required, not optional

The Codex `Run()` normalizes the OpenAI usage block into `pkg/dispatch.Usage`,
or budget enforcement, `tide_cost_cents_total`, and the cache-efficiency panel
all read $0/blank for Codex:

- `InputTokens`  ← `usage.prompt_tokens`
- `OutputTokens` ← `usage.completion_tokens`
- `CacheReadTokens` ← `usage.prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens`
  (OpenAI caching is automatic, marker-free, 1,024-token floor — there is no
  `cache_control` to set and no separate "cache creation" concept, so
  **`CacheCreationTokens` stays 0**, unlike Anthropic.)
- `EstimatedCostCents` + `CacheSavingsCents` ← a Codex-side pricing table
  mirroring `anthropic/pricing.go` (per-model cents/MTok; `cacheRead` is
  OpenAI's discounted cached-input rate).

The locked metric label set stays `{project,phase,plan,wave}`; the `task` label
remains **forbidden** (metriccardinality analyzer). The provider-neutral
`SharedContext` field on `EnvelopeIn` is plain Go struct state — Codex receives
it for free, no special handling.

### D6 — Codex CLI invocation (carried verbatim — do not re-research)

`codex exec` is first-class headless (one-shot, no TTY, final message to
stdout). Container-clean flags: `--ephemeral --skip-git-repo-check
--ignore-user-config`. `--json` emits a JSONL event stream (parsed for the
usage block + final message). `--output-schema <file>` constrains the final
response to a JSON schema — used for the mandated JSON child-CRD emission.
Sandbox defaults read-only; the runner sets `--sandbox workspace-write` (safe
inside an already-isolated pod, mirrors the Claude runner posture). Auth is
env-key (`OPENAI_API_KEY`). Image name mirrors the Claude convention:
`ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent` (exact tag/registry are authoring
decisions).
EOF

## Phase Decomposition

Six phases. All cross-phase interface contracts — the `"openai"` vendor
sentinel, the `Usage` field mapping, the vendor precedence chain, the image
name, and the wave-failure contract — are locked in this document, so the
planning DAG is shallow. Phase 01 (pricing/usage) and phase 04 (vendor switch)
have no dependencies and dispatch together; phase 02 builds on 01, phase 05 on
04, phase 03 on 02; phase 06 is the milestone-wide join barrier.

```mermaid
flowchart LR

    P2[phase-02-codex-subagent-core]
    P3[phase-03-codex-image-entrypoint]
    P4[phase-04-per-level-vendor-switch]
    P5[phase-05-chart-secret-wiring]
    P6[phase-06-hetero-integration]

```

### phase-01-codex-pricing-usage — *(no dependencies)*

Pure-Go foundation for Codex cost/usage. Adds `internal/subagent/codex/pricing.go`
(per-model cents/MTok table mirroring `anthropic/pricing.go`; `cacheRead` is the
discounted cached-input rate; no `cacheWrite` concept) and the usage-normalization
helper that maps an OpenAI usage block into `pkg/dispatch.Usage` per D5
(`cached_tokens → CacheReadTokens`, `CacheCreationTokens` fixed at 0,
`EstimatedCostCents` + `CacheSavingsCents` computed). Deliverable boundary:
`pricing_test.go` proves the table and cost math offline, including the
1,024-token cache floor and the zero-cache-creation invariant. No CLI, no
container, no controller touch.

### phase-02-codex-subagent-core — *(depends on: phase-01-codex-pricing-usage)*

The core `internal/subagent/codex/` package behind `pkg/dispatch.Subagent`:
`client.go` (Client + constructor), `run.go` (`Run` shells `codex exec` with the
D6 flag set, schema-constrained child-CRD emission via `--output-schema`),
`stream_parser.go` (JSONL event stream → final message + usage block),
`doc.go`. Fail-fast vendor check: refuse any envelope whose `Provider.Vendor !=
"openai"`, mirroring the anthropic sentinel guard. Populates `Usage` via the
phase-01 helper. Deliverable boundary: `Run` unit-tested against a fake `codex`
binary / fixture JSONL stream — fully offline; any real OpenAI call is guarded
behind `OPENAI_API_KEY` being set. Child-CRD read/traversal defenses mirror
anthropic's. No controller, no chart, no `cmd/` changes.
EOF

## Exit Criteria

1. `make test` exits 0 — all existing unit and integration tests pass unmodified.

   (fail-fast vendor guard, unit test).
5. The Codex usage normalizer maps `cached_tokens → CacheReadTokens`, holds
   `CacheCreationTokens == 0`, and the pricing table computes
   `EstimatedCostCents`/`CacheSavingsCents` offline (unit test).
6. `internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile` builds clean and bundles the Codex CLI.
7. The mixed-provider integration run reaches completion; a forced Codex task
   failure reproduces the D4 wave-boundary semantics byte-for-byte.
8. `tide_cost_cents_total` and the cache-efficiency panel attribute non-zero
   Codex spend under the `{project,phase,plan,wave}` label set; the `task` label
   is absent (metriccardinality analyzer green).
9. No OpenAI API call runs in tests unless `OPENAI_API_KEY` is set — the suite
   passes fully offline.

    are referenced by Secret name only.

- All Codex/OpenAI-specific code stays in `internal/subagent/codex/` behind the
  `pkg/dispatch.Subagent` interface — no provider leakage into the controller
  or `cmd/manager`.
- Wave-boundary failure semantics unchanged for mixed-provider waves; no
  parallel failure path — the global indegree model is authoritative.
- Secret material never inlined in manifests or chart values — Secret name only.

  guards green.

  delivered.
- No real OpenAI API call in tests without an explicit `OPENAI_API_KEY` env
  guard — tests pass offline.

- **Upstream:** v1.0.2 "Spring Tide" + late v1.0.1 — the v1alpha2 schema +

- **Downstream:** None currently scoped. The vendor switch generalizes to

# Milestone 03 — Heterogeneous Provider Dispatch (Codex Runtime)

**Project:** dogfood-codex-runtime
**Slug:** `milestone-03-hetero-integration`
**Status:** Planned — phases dispatched

---

## Outcome Statement

A TIDE operator configures a single Project whose **planner pool runs Claude**
and whose **executor pool runs Codex (OpenAI)**, applies it, and watches a
multi-phase run dispatch to completion across both providers in one set of
Project-scoped waves. Mixed-provider waves actually execute — not interface
scaffolding — and the wave-boundary failure contract is byte-for-byte identical
regardless of which provider ran a task. Codex spend is attributed correctly on
the Telemetry tab and counts against the same budget cap as Claude spend.

The only new product surface is the **Codex subagent package**
(`internal/subagent/codex/`) plus the **per-level vendor switch** in the
controller. Everything else — the global Execution DAG, wave derivation, global
dispatch/failure/gates/resumption, cost/budget/telemetry/cache, and the
dashboard — already ships in main and is consumed unchanged.

---

## Architecture Decisions (locked — do not reinvent in phases)

### D1 — Implementation location mirrors `internal/subagent/anthropic/` exactly

`internal/subagent/codex/` ships `client.go` (Client + constructor), `run.go`
(`Subagent.Run(ctx, EnvelopeIn) (EnvelopeOut, error)`), a JSONL `stream_parser.go`,
`pricing.go`, `doc.go` (references the D-C1 layering pattern), and `Dockerfile`
(multi-stage, mirrors `anthropic/Dockerfile`). All OpenAI/Codex-specific code
stays behind the `pkg/dispatch.Subagent` interface. **No Codex imports** in
`internal/controller/` or `cmd/manager/` — enforced by the existing provider
import firewall (`make verify-import-firewall`, `verify-dispatch-imports`).

### D2 — Per-level runtime selection (not per-task, not per-project)

The planner pool and the executor pool each pin a runtime via the existing
`Spec.Subagent.Levels.{milestone,phase,plan,task}` overrides. This is the
correct granularity: it matches the spec's separate-pools argument, achieves
real heterogeneity within one run, and avoids the API/scheduler complexity of
per-task selection. Rejected alternatives (do not reopen): per-task selection
(large API surface, scheduler churn) and per-project selection (no mixing within
a run, defeats the goal).

Today `resolveImage` already honors a per-level image, but `ResolveProvider`
(`internal/controller/dispatch_helpers.go`) returns `Vendor:"anthropic"`
unconditionally — so a Codex image is *selected* at a level but then *rejected*
at runtime by the image's vendor sentinel. **Wiring the per-level vendor switch
is part of this milestone.** Vendor resolves down the same precedence chain as
model/image: `Levels.<level>.Vendor → Spec.Subagent.Vendor → Helm default →
"anthropic"`. The Codex subagent's compiled-in sentinel is `"openai"`.

### D3 — Credential path: Secret-ref only, env-key auth

`OPENAI_API_KEY` (or `CODEX_API_KEY`) is delivered via a same-namespace K8s
Secret using the established `providerSecretRef` pattern. Never mount host
config, never headless OAuth (broken upstream: claude-code#29983, #7100 — the
same restriction applies to Codex). Secret material is **referenced by name
only**; chart values carry the Secret name, never the key bytes.

### D4 — Mixed-provider waves: identical wave-boundary failure semantics

The spec's "Failure handling at wave boundaries" applies unchanged across
providers and is already enforced by the global indegree model:

- Failed task → siblings in the same wave continue (declared independent).
- Dependents in later waves → never dispatched (indegree never reaches zero).
- Non-dependents in later waves → dispatched in strict profile.

This contract **must not change** when tasks run on different providers. No
parallel failure path is added — the global indegree model is the single
source of truth.

### D5 — Usage accounting + cost are required, not optional

The Codex `Run()` normalizes the OpenAI usage block into `pkg/dispatch.Usage`,
or budget enforcement, `tide_cost_cents_total`, and the cache-efficiency panel
all read $0/blank for Codex:

- `InputTokens`  ← `usage.prompt_tokens`
- `OutputTokens` ← `usage.completion_tokens`
- `CacheReadTokens` ← `usage.prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens`
  (OpenAI caching is automatic, marker-free, 1,024-token floor — there is no
  `cache_control` to set and no separate "cache creation" concept, so
  **`CacheCreationTokens` stays 0**, unlike Anthropic.)
- `EstimatedCostCents` + `CacheSavingsCents` ← a Codex-side pricing table
  mirroring `anthropic/pricing.go` (per-model cents/MTok; `cacheRead` is
  OpenAI's discounted cached-input rate).

The locked metric label set stays `{project,phase,plan,wave}`; the `task` label
remains **forbidden** (metriccardinality analyzer). The provider-neutral
`SharedContext` field on `EnvelopeIn` is plain Go struct state — Codex receives
it for free, no special handling.

### D6 — Codex CLI invocation (carried verbatim — do not re-research)

`codex exec` is first-class headless (one-shot, no TTY, final message to
stdout). Container-clean flags: `--ephemeral --skip-git-repo-check
--ignore-user-config`. `--json` emits a JSONL event stream (parsed for the
usage block + final message). `--output-schema <file>` constrains the final
response to a JSON schema — used for the mandated JSON child-CRD emission.
Sandbox defaults read-only; the runner sets `--sandbox workspace-write` (safe
inside an already-isolated pod, mirrors the Claude runner posture). Auth is
env-key (`OPENAI_API_KEY`). Image name mirrors the Claude convention:
`ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent` (exact tag/registry are authoring
decisions).

---

## Phase Decomposition

Six phases. All cross-phase interface contracts — the `"openai"` vendor
sentinel, the `Usage` field mapping, the vendor precedence chain, the image
name, and the wave-failure contract — are locked in this document, so the
planning DAG is shallow. Phase 01 (pricing/usage) and phase 04 (vendor switch)
have no dependencies and dispatch together; phase 02 builds on 01, phase 05 on
04, phase 03 on 02; phase 06 is the milestone-wide join barrier.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    P1[phase-01-codex-pricing-usage]
    P2[phase-02-codex-subagent-core]
    P3[phase-03-codex-image-entrypoint]
    P4[phase-04-per-level-vendor-switch]
    P5[phase-05-chart-secret-wiring]
    P6[phase-06-hetero-integration]
    P1 --> P2
    P2 --> P3
    P4 --> P5
    P2 --> P6
    P3 --> P6
    P4 --> P6
    P5 --> P6
```

### phase-01-codex-pricing-usage — *(no dependencies)*

Pure-Go foundation for Codex cost/usage. Adds `internal/subagent/codex/pricing.go`
(per-model cents/MTok table mirroring `anthropic/pricing.go`; `cacheRead` is the
discounted cached-input rate; no `cacheWrite` concept) and the usage-normalization
helper that maps an OpenAI usage block into `pkg/dispatch.Usage` per D5
(`cached_tokens → CacheReadTokens`, `CacheCreationTokens` fixed at 0,
`EstimatedCostCents` + `CacheSavingsCents` computed). Deliverable boundary:
`pricing_test.go` proves the table and cost math offline, including the
1,024-token cache floor and the zero-cache-creation invariant. No CLI, no
container, no controller touch.

### phase-02-codex-subagent-core — *(depends on: phase-01-codex-pricing-usage)*

The core `internal/subagent/codex/` package behind `pkg/dispatch.Subagent`:
`client.go` (Client + constructor), `run.go` (`Run` shells `codex exec` with the
D6 flag set, schema-constrained child-CRD emission via `--output-schema`),
`stream_parser.go` (JSONL event stream → final message + usage block),
`doc.go`. Fail-fast vendor check: refuse any envelope whose `Provider.Vendor !=
"openai"`, mirroring the anthropic sentinel guard. Populates `Usage` via the
phase-01 helper. Deliverable boundary: `Run` unit-tested against a fake `codex`
binary / fixture JSONL stream — fully offline; any real OpenAI call is guarded
behind `OPENAI_API_KEY` being set. Child-CRD read/traversal defenses mirror
anthropic's. No controller, no chart, no `cmd/` changes.

### phase-03-codex-image-entrypoint — *(depends on: phase-02-codex-subagent-core)*

The runtime image and its entrypoint. Adds `cmd/codex-subagent/main.go` (thin
shim: load `EnvelopeIn` → `codex.Run` → write `EnvelopeOut`, mirroring
`cmd/claude-subagent/main.go`, harness-wrapped) and
`internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile` (multi-stage build bundling the Codex CLI
binary, mirroring `anthropic/Dockerfile`). Deliverable boundary: the Dockerfile
builds clean; the entrypoint round-trips a fixture envelope through the package
offline; image-build/load tooling references
`ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent`. Imports the phase-02 package only.

### phase-04-per-level-vendor-switch — *(no dependencies)*

The controller-side vendor switch — no Codex imports. Adds a `Vendor` field to
`SubagentConfig`/`LevelConfig` (CEL-validated, optional, defaults to
`"anthropic"`), regenerates CRDs/deepcopy, and rewires `ResolveProvider`
(`dispatch_helpers.go`) to resolve vendor down the precedence chain
`Levels.<level>.Vendor → Spec.Subagent.Vendor → Helm default → "anthropic"`,
replacing the unconditional `Vendor:"anthropic"`. Threads the Helm vendor
default through `ProviderDefaults` + `cmd/manager/env.go`. Deliverable boundary:
unit tests prove a Codex level resolves `Vendor:"openai"` while a Claude level
stays `"anthropic"` in the same Project; `make manifests`/`generate` clean;
`verify-import-firewall` + `verify-dispatch-imports` green (zero provider
leakage). The interface contract — the `"openai"` sentinel string — matches
phase-02 by the lock in this doc, not by a code edge.

### phase-05-chart-secret-wiring — *(depends on: phase-04-per-level-vendor-switch)*

Helm chart + operator-facing config. Adds the `images.codexSubagent` /
`subagent.defaults` block for the Codex image, the new provider-Secret **name**
value for `OPENAI_API_KEY`/`CODEX_API_KEY` (referenced by name only — never the
key bytes), the env-key injection into dispatch Job pods following the
`providerSecretRef` pattern, and the manager-Deployment env that feeds the
phase-04 Helm vendor default. Deliverable boundary: `helm template` renders the
codex image default, the Secret ref, and the vendor-default env across
enabled/disabled permutations; no secret material inlined. Consumes the phase-04
manager-env contract.

### phase-06-hetero-integration — *(depends on: phase-02, phase-03, phase-04, phase-05)*

The milestone's join barrier — authors no new feature scope. Adds the
heterogeneous example Project (`examples/projects/.../project.yaml`: planner
pool Claude, executor pool Codex) and the mixed-provider integration test that
dispatches a multi-phase run to completion across both providers in one set of
Project-scoped waves. Verifies, end-to-end: (1) the wave-boundary failure
contract (D4) is identical when the failed task ran on Codex — siblings
continue, dependents never dispatch, non-dependents dispatch in strict profile;
(2) Codex spend is attributed on `tide_cost_cents_total` + the cache-efficiency
panel under the locked label set, and counts against the budget cap; (3)
`make test` exits 0 and every `make verify-*` guard is green. Fixes whatever the
join surfaces.

---

## Exit Criteria

1. `make test` exits 0 — all existing unit and integration tests pass unmodified.
2. `make verify-import-firewall` and `make verify-dispatch-imports` are green:
   no `internal/subagent/codex` (or OpenAI SDK) import appears in
   `internal/controller/` or `cmd/manager/`.
3. In one Project, `ResolveProvider` returns `Vendor:"openai"` for a
   Codex-pinned level and `Vendor:"anthropic"` for a Claude-pinned level
   (unit test).
4. The Codex `Run()` rejects an envelope whose `Provider.Vendor != "openai"`
   (fail-fast vendor guard, unit test).
5. The Codex usage normalizer maps `cached_tokens → CacheReadTokens`, holds
   `CacheCreationTokens == 0`, and the pricing table computes
   `EstimatedCostCents`/`CacheSavingsCents` offline (unit test).
6. `internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile` builds clean and bundles the Codex CLI.
7. The mixed-provider integration run reaches completion; a forced Codex task
   failure reproduces the D4 wave-boundary semantics byte-for-byte.
8. `tide_cost_cents_total` and the cache-efficiency panel attribute non-zero
   Codex spend under the `{project,phase,plan,wave}` label set; the `task` label
   is absent (metriccardinality analyzer green).
9. No OpenAI API call runs in tests unless `OPENAI_API_KEY` is set — the suite
   passes fully offline.
10. No secret material is inlined in any manifest or chart value — credentials
    are referenced by Secret name only.

---

## Constraints (inherited)

- All Codex/OpenAI-specific code stays in `internal/subagent/codex/` behind the
  `pkg/dispatch.Subagent` interface — no provider leakage into the controller
  or `cmd/manager`.
- Wave-boundary failure semantics unchanged for mixed-provider waves; no
  parallel failure path — the global indegree model is authoritative.
- Secret material never inlined in manifests or chart values — Secret name only.
- Apache 2.0 headers on all new files, logr/zap logging, CEL validation for new
  CRD fields, controller-runtime patterns throughout; keep all `make verify-*`
  guards green.
- Do not break existing tests; confirm `make test` is green before any plan is
  delivered.
- No real OpenAI API call in tests without an explicit `OPENAI_API_KEY` env
  guard — tests pass offline.

---

## Upstream / Downstream Milestones

- **Upstream:** v1.0.2 "Spring Tide" + late v1.0.1 — the v1alpha2 schema +
  global Execution DAG (Phases 23–26), global dispatch/failure/gates/resumption
  (Phase 25), cost/budget/telemetry/cache (Phases 16, 18–21), and the dashboard
  global execution-DAG view (Phase 26). All consumed as given infrastructure.
- **Downstream:** None currently scoped. The vendor switch generalizes to
  additional providers (Google, xAI) by adding a subagent package + pricing
  table behind the same interface — no controller change required.
MILESTONE_EOF
